steps.title: Interpret 125% more as a growth factor
steps.content:
If the population increases by 125%, the new population equals the old population plus 125% of the old population.

If there are $N$ rabbits, the new count is $N + 1.25N = 2.25N$.

Therefore, each year the population is multiplied by a factor of 2.25, which gives an exponential model: $n = a(2.25)^t$, where $a$ is the population when $t = 0$ (end of 2016).

steps.title: Use the given data point to find the initial value
steps.content:
The end of 2017 is $t = 1$ year after the end of 2016. We are told that $n = 180$ at this time. Substituting into our model:
$$180 = a(2.25)^1$$
$$180 = 2.25a$$
$$a = \frac{180}{2.25} = 80$$

steps.title: Write and identify the equation
steps.content: With $a = 80$, the equation becomes $n = 80(2.25)^t$. That matches choice B, so B is correct.
